title: Salty Crispy Saudi-Arabian Date-Night Tagine
description: Savor the combination of rich Thai chili jam and crispy, caramelized onions in this Saudi-Arabian-inspired date-night dish, perfect for a romantic evening in.
instructions: Season the chicken thighs with cumin, coriander, kosher salt, and black pepper.
Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the chicken thighs and sear until browned on both sides, about 5-6 minutes per side.
Transfer the chicken to a plate and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil. Add the sliced onions and cook until caramelized and dark brown, about 20-25 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the Thai chili jam and cook for 1-2 minutes, until heated through.
Add the chopped dates and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until they start to soften.
Add the chicken back into the skillet and spoon some of the chili jam and onion mixture over the top of each thigh.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 15-20 minutes, until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce is bubbly.
While the chicken is cooking, prepare an ice bath by filling a large bowl with ice and water.
Once the chicken is done, remove it from the oven and immediately submerge it in the ice bath to stop the cooking process.
Let the chicken cool in the ice bath for 5-10 minutes, until it reaches an internal temperature of 165°F (74°C).
Remove the chicken from the ice bath and garnish with chopped fresh parsley and sliced almonds.
Serve the chicken thighs hot, spooning some of the chili jam and onion mixture over the top of each thigh.
Garnish with additional parsley and almonds, if desired.
To plate, place a chicken thigh on each plate and spoon some of the chili jam and onion mixture over the top.
Garnish with a sprinkle of kosher salt and a few slices of fresh parsley.
